seoTitle: How to Connect QuickBooks to PayPal
metaDescription: Learn how to connect QuickBooks to PayPal so your transactions sync automatically and your accounting and payment systems stay in step.
intro: Keeping PayPal and QuickBooks in sync means fewer manual entries and more accurate books. This guide walks through connecting the two platforms so your PayPal transactions flow into QuickBooks for reconciliation.

steps.heading: Find the PayPal connection option
steps.body: Go to your bank feed or app connections in QuickBooks Online and search for PayPal.

steps.heading: Sign in and authorize the connection
steps.body: Log in to your PayPal account when prompted and grant QuickBooks permission to access your transaction data.

steps.heading: Set the sync date range
steps.body: Choose how far back you want transactions imported so you don't end up duplicating older entries.

steps.heading: Review imported transactions
steps.body: Check the imported PayPal transactions in your bank feed and categorize them correctly.

faq.question: Does connecting PayPal cost extra in QuickBooks?
faq.answer: The connection itself is generally included, though PayPal's own transaction fees still apply as usual.

faq.question: How often does the sync update?
faq.answer: Once connected, PayPal transactions typically sync automatically on a regular schedule rather than needing manual refreshes.

faq.question: Can I disconnect PayPal later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can remove the connection from your bank feed settings at any time.

faq.question: Will PayPal fees be separated from sales automatically?
faq.answer: Depending on how the feed is set up, you may need to split gross transactions into sales and fees during categorization.
